title: List of dairy products
rows: An Indian dessert mostly in Bihar , Maharashtra , Gujarat and Karnataka . It is a sweetened dense milk made by boiling milk on low heat until the milk is reduced by half
A type of khoa specially linked to city of Khan garh in Pakistan
A fermented milk product made from whey . It is similar in alcohol content to wine
Prepared from yogurt , its production involves several processes
An elastic , sticky , high level melt resistant ice cream which should delay melting in the hotter climates of the Arabic countries where it is most commonly found
Yoghurt drink mixed with coriander , mustard seeds , mint and other spices
A traditional and nutritious dairy product prepared from buffalo milk and it is popular throughout south Asian countries such as India , Pakistan , Sri Lanka and Nepal

A fermented milk product . In common with all dairy yogurt , Bulgarian yogurt is produced through the bacterial fermentation of milk , using a live culture of Lactobacillus bulgaricus and Streptococcus thermophilus

Made by churning fresh or fermented cream or milk . It is generally used as a spread and a condiment , as well as in cooking , such as baking , sauce making , and pan frying . Butter consists of butterfat , milk proteins and water . See also Hard sauce
The fatty portion of milk . Milk and cream are often sold according to the amount of butterfat they contain
Refers to a number of dairy drinks . Originally , buttermilk was the liquid left behind after churning butter out of cream . This type of buttermilk is known as traditional buttermilk
A sweet cold beverage or soup , made with buttermilk and other ingredients . Pictured is buttermilk koldskål with biscuits

Used in the production of ice cream as a source of solids , in processed sliced cheese to increase viscosity , as an emulsifier in chocolate products and in dry mixes such as pancake mix , to add dairy flavor and enhance food browning

description: This table provides a list of dairy products, detailing their names, origins, and descriptions. It serves as a reference for understanding various dairy items and their cultural or geographical significance. The table is structured to highlight the diversity of dairy products across different regions.
